programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine receives a visitor, Nicola is left frustrated, and Ross and Harvey hatch a plan.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tensions are high as Jasmine’s trial commences, George makes a declaration, and Ross grows paranoid.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jasmine faces a crucial decision, Penny confronts Nicola, and Ross and Vicki discuss their future.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The verdict in Jasmine’s trial is read, Vicki shares her hesitations, and Elaine and Ian hold their campaign launch events.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Twickenham to help the Allan family tackle their overflowing home.

programme.title: Tenet
programme.desc: A hardened CIA agent is recruited into a mind-bending mystery with cause and effect ‘inverted’, so weapons can strike from the future. His suspect is ruthless Russian oligarch Andrei Sator. Globe-trotting espionage drama with a science-fiction edge from multi-award winning Christopher Nolan, director of Inception, Oppenheimer and Interstellar.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. She goes to a high-octane pool party to discover how casinos are cashing in on the party scene.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir goes even further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting those who live and work there.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: An investigation into a viral prison sex tape leads journalist Mobeen Azhar to discover an illicit prison economy fuelled by inappropriate relationships, coercion and contraband.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Mobeen’s investigation into prison corruption hears of the murder of a former officer who had been involved in uncovering an illicit relationship between a guard and an inmate.

programme.title: Press X on the BBC
programme.desc: Eight games, a book and a luxury. What would you choose? Steffan Powell chats with gaming YouTuber Elz the Witch and gaming experts and comedians Inel Tomlinson and Glenn Moore. Like the programme? Listen to more on the Press X To Continue podcast, available on BBC Sounds.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall delves into the weird and wonderful world of parasites in this Deadly 60 special. From leeches to tapeworms and cuckoos to mosquitoes he reveals the mind-boggling methods that parasites employ to survive, breed and feed by exploiting their animal and human hosts.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: This time, Steve and the crew travel to Venezuela in search of more lethal animal predators for their Deadly list. First, they comb the Venezuelan wetlands looking for the biggest snake in the world - the mighty green anaconda, capable of growing to over six metres in length. Then Steve heads beneath the rainforest into a dark cave infested with vampire bats, where a true toxic terror lurks - the scolopendra, a giant centipede armed with a powerful venomous bite.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James attempt to save the planet by building their own electric car and then put their latest 'green' creation through the rigours of the new car testing process. Meanwhile on the track, it is the battle of the continents as Europe takes on the USA and Jeremy pits the Corvette ZR1's muscle against the finesse of Audi's R8 V10. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Frost/Nixon and The Damned United star Michael Sheen.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: Los Angeles is home to more than a quarter of a million dogs - from the pampered pooches of Beverley Hills to the pit bull terriers of the projects. Louis Theroux enters the bizarre world of LA's dogs, meeting the characters whose lives revolve around the city's huge canine population. In the toughest neighbourhoods of south LA, he has a terrifying encounter with a 'weaponised' dog, trained to attack on command, and goes on patrol with 'Dogman', whose mission is to rescue some of the thousands of stray and feral pit bulls roaming the streets. At the city's biggest animal shelter, Louis meets the hard-pressed staff, whose heartbreaking job includes euthanizing thousands of unwanted dogs every year. He also encounters the middle-class Americans who adopt some of these delinquent dogs, as well as the colourful dog-training gurus who claim to be able to take the ghetto out of the dog once the dog has been rescued from the streets.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: Louis visits LA's most famous hospital, the Cedars-Sinai Medical Centre in West Hollywood, where he meets patients battling for their lives against serious illnesses. America spends a huge amount on health care and a hefty proportion goes on end of life care, with a wide and expensive array of treatments on offer to those with insurance or who qualify for government funded programmes. Louis talks to patients and their families as they face some of the toughest decisions imaginable - whether to accept death and die in relative comfort, or to gamble on further, possibly painful treatment in the hope that it will cure them or prolong their life. He also talks to the doctors whose job it is to guide people through this traumatic process. Over several months Louis explores the American way of death, which often involves never giving up and keeping faith right to the end.

programme.title: Louis Theroux's LA Stories
programme.desc: In the final episode of Louis Theroux's LA Stories, Louis spends time in the twilight world of the city's paroled sex offenders, whose crimes have made them pariahs living on the fringes of society. The State of California has some of the toughest regulations anywhere in America controlling sex offenders. On leaving prison they can spend years on parole, tagged and tracked, and a lifetime on an online sex offenders register. Louis spent several months at some of the hostels in Los Angeles where sex offenders live, talking in depth to sex offenders themselves as well as the people charged with monitoring them. The tough rules governing where they can live and who they can see make it hard for registered sex offenders to find work or even housing, and many are forced into homelessness. Meeting these outcasts Louis asks whether these tough rules keep society safer and when, if ever, it is right to give sex offenders a second chance.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Aged 19 and bored of where she grew up, Michaella craved sun and adventure. But her dream turned into a nightmare as she fell into an illicit world of drugs and excess.

programme.title: High
programme.desc: Michaella tells the story of finding herself 5,000 miles from home in Peru, pretending to be a tourist at Machu Picchu, then walking 12kg of cocaine into an airport.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from London and south east England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges: Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian and all-round food enthusiast Phil Wang. The guest judge is Simon Callow, the much-loved actor who has starred in such box office hits as Shakespeare in Love, A Room with a View and Four Weddings and a Funeral. How will he judge dishes celebrating the British film industry? Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent London and south east England at the national finals.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: A new generation of big cats is coming of age. First-time lion mum Serami’s tiny cubs surprise the crew and could mark a split in the Xudum pride. It’s time for Mathata and the oldest cubs in the pride to pull their weight. Pobe the cheetah makes a welcome return with her surviving cub Bo, who finally shows the team what he’s made of. Leopard mum Lediba’s female cub is becoming increasingly independent. But her new-found confidence panics her mum and the crew. And a new character bursts onto the scene, bringing even more tension to the delta.

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Mary joins the Gardiners in the Lake District, hoping for clarity and calm. She reconnects with Mr Hayward, and they bond over poetry and nature. But just as it seems as though they might finally declare their feelings for one another, Mr Ryder arrives to disrupt proceedings, with Caroline Bingley in tow. Once again, Mary’s hopes hang in the balance.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, the Eye team investigates Chinese disciplinary schools promising to ‘fix’ problem children at a devastating cost. And we’ll hear how a growing number of girls In India are challenging the practice of child marriage, using sport to forge their own futures.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Brinicle invites his Piri friends round to taste his brilliant brioche. Yuk - it’s chewy and hasn't risen at all! Despite this, Brinicle announces he has entered a baking competition. A disaster looms, but Flutter has an idea.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Cosima and Big Lizard investigate the cause of Dad's mysterious sleepwalking in an adventure that takes them around Proxima and right back home again.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: While playing baseball, Weasy hits a ball out of bounds. It lands on a scary scarecrow, which frightens the ducklings. Weasy makes fun of them, but he's scared, too! He leaves without the ball but refuses to admit that he’s afraid.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Much to Trevor’s irritation, a rare phoenix woodpecker takes a liking to him - so he demands an immediate quest to return the bird to its real home.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play marbles, and it's driving Grizzy crazy.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck has a job punting tourists along the waterways, but a bored Frog thinks he can do a better job. Thanks to Frog's interference, the pair wind up going on a wild trip down the rapids.

programme.title: Game on Grandparents
programme.desc: The winning teams return to the arena to take on Mario Kart in the Grand Final. Who will win the competition and claim the Game on Grandparents trophy and GOAT title?

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe enter a gaming world and embark on a quest to unearth treasure before attending Sharky’s very chaotic party.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro and Dame Maggie Aderin-Pocock team up for an out-of-this-world space prank on Comet. Prepare for wind machines, cosmic rubber chickens and an unexpected meteor shower!

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Timor Leste in search of the biggest animal to have ever lived - the blue whale. He also hopes to find the saltwater crocodile, one of the giants of land.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Spring is bursting into life as Frances Tophill returns to beautiful Damson Farm, where the garden is waking up in spectacular style. She shares important research from the RSPB on how to urgently support garden birds, reveals how to make the most of sowing older seeds, and gets hands-on preparing vibrant container displays ready for the sunny days ahead. At home, Toby Buckland is brimming with ingenious ideas to help you get the most from your outdoor space. Meanwhile, Joe Swift travels to the Cornish coast to meet a gardener who proves that even the wildest seaside weather can’t stop a true planting paradise. There's also a visit to a Cheshire gardener whose passion for Erythronium has created a display that’s genuinely out of this world, plus a viewer in Birmingham who has transformed their garden into a heartfelt celebration of daffodils.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Seven well known personalities of differing faiths and beliefs set off on a unique 390km series of trails through the stunning landscapes of north east England to explore the lives of early Celtic Christian saints. They trek through the heart of Northumberland and the Scottish Borders along a curated network of trails celebrating three of England’s seventh-century northern saints – Hild, Oswald and Cuthbert. During the journey, they encounter Whitby’s famous cliff-top abbey and two of the most important pilgrimage sites in north east England – the Unesco site of Durham Cathedral and their final destination, the Holy Island of Lindisfarne.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims continue their adventure in Durham and visit the 12th-century Norman cathedral. They are met by Canon Charlie Allen, who introduces them to the ancient pilgrim tradition of foot washing, although not all the pilgrims are keen to proffer their naked feet. Leaving Durham behind them, they head for Heavenfield, an ancient battlefield, and Housesteads, the UK’s best-preserved Roman fort on Hadrian’s Wall. Some of them head to Newcastle to visit the city's Reform synagogue. For another taste of hermit life, the pilgrims follow St Oswald’s Way towards a hermitage from the early 1400s. Belonging to Warkworth Castle, the hermitage is carved deep inside a rock. It has its own tiny chapel and is where a hermit priest once lived. Along the way, the pilgrims start to open up and reveal more of themselves as the journey continues.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for London and south east England with blockbuster canapes, starters and fish dishes celebrating the British film industry. The dishes are judged by this week's veteran judge, Michelin-starred chef and former banquet fish course winner Aktar Islam, and include a Paddington-inspired take on savoury marmalade and a stuffed poached brill with a Vesper martini broth in a nod to Casino Royale. Who will impress, and who will be sent home?

programme.title: Rugby League: Challenge Cup
programme.desc: Hull Kingston Rovers face York Knights in the Rugby League Challenge Cup quarter-finals.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode comes from Southwell Minster in the town of Southwell near Nottingham, where experts Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey find antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Paul Martin finds out how Southwell Minster has had a cavalcade of historical figures pass through the doors, from Cardinal Wolsey to Charles I, at some dramatic moments in British history. Paul also explores Newstead Abbey, the country seat of poet and renowned Lothario Lord Byron, and finds out there is more to him and his relationships than meets the eye.

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Rugby
programme.desc: The first round of the 2026 Women's Six Nations finishes with Wales v Scotland in Cardiff.

programme.title: My Hero
programme.desc: Miranda Hart explores the life of her comedy idol, Eric Morecambe. She visits the places he performed, meets the people who knew him and shares Morecambe and Wise performances that influenced her own work. Featuring rare footage and much-loved Morecambe and Wise gems, her journey takes her across Britain, from deepest Wales to meet comedy legend Eddie Braben; to the Essex studio of the artist who painted Eric at the height of his fame.

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Show
programme.desc: As a treat for comedy fans, this is a complete episode of The Morecambe and Wise Show that had been considered lost for over 50 years. Eric’s son Gary Morecambe discovered the programme in an unmarked film can in 2020, and it has been lovingly restored. The show, written by the legendary Eddie Braben, was episode 1 of Eric and Ernie’s first series to appear on BBC1 and was originally watched by over 14 million viewers. As well as some classic sketches from Eric and Ernie, the show also features songs and music from Paul Anka, Patricia Lambert, Kenny Ball and his Jazzmen, and the boys themselves rounding things off with a burst of Bring Me Sunshine.

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of McCartney’s original Höfner bass over 50 years ago is one of rock ‘n’ roll’s great mysteries. This is the story of the iconic instrument and the quest to find it. Featuring exclusive interviews with Paul McCartney, his brother Mike, Klaus Voormann, Elvis Costello and the roadies, journalists and fans who embarked on a mission to return the bass to its place in music history.

programme.title: Paul McCartney at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of TV appearances from the post-Beatles career of Britain’s most successful singer-songwriter. This playlist shows how the Fab Four’s split didn’t stop the hits and features some of Paul McCartney’s most popular songs and iconic performances on some of the biggest BBC shows of the past five decades, from Top of the Pops to the Electric Proms. Alongside tracks like Band on the Run, Jet, Coming Up and Live and Let Die, there is rarely seen backstage footage, interviews and a range of familiar faces, including a recent encounter with die-hard Beatles fan Bob Mortimer.

programme.title: ... Sings The Beatles
programme.desc: Recorded for the fortieth anniversary of Abbey Road, The Beatles' penultimate album, a journey through the classic and curious covers in the BBC archives. Featuring Sandie Shaw singing a sassy Day Tripper, Shirley Bassey belting out Something, a close-harmony Carpenters cover of Help!, Joe Cocker's chart-topping With a Little Help from My Friends, Oasis reinventing I Am the Walrus and a little Lady Madonna from Macca himself. Plus, a few 'magical' moments from Candy Flip, The Korean Kittens and Su Pollard.

programme.title: One Fine Morning
programme.desc: Working as a freelance interpreter, single mother Sandra lives with her eight-year-old daughter in Paris. As she comes to terms with the painful deterioration of her father, who is suffering from a rare form of dementia, she runs into Clement, an old friend, and they embark on an affair. In French with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Carey and Pierson escape into a network of underground tunnels as Pierson’s past comes back to haunt him. Unarmed and with no back-up, Carey has no choice but to hear Pierson’s story. She is horrified by what Pierson has been through and the extent of the conspiracy that they are now both entangled in. Simon's real identity is revealed as Carey’s allies above ground piece together the truth about Pierson. Carey forges an uneasy alliance to bring down the forces truly responsible for Isaac Turner’s murder.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor visit Bisterne Estate in Hampshire’s Avon Valley, where farmland and water meadows provide a vital home for two classic signs of spring – the lapwing and the hare. They meet the farmers and conservationists working together to restore habitats for both species, while Matt records the sounds of spring with a local sound artist and joins volunteers caring for the historic Harnham Water Meadows. Author Robert Macfarlane shares an exclusive spring reading, and on his Cotswolds farm, Adam Henson restores a traditional hedgerow, just in time for nesting birds.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: As Landward continues to mark its 50th anniversary, Dougie is at Inveraray Castle in Argyle, where he looks back at the devastating fire that gutted the castle in 1975 and hears how it was saved with help from local people. Elsewhere, Cammy is watching the action at the Stirling Bull Sales to find out why some animals fetch record prices. Arlene revisits a story from the Landward archive, following in the footsteps of presenter Ken Rundle as he found out about a new farm diversification known as agritourism. Meanwhile, Leanna takes a walk through an East Sutherland woodland to discover what there is to like about lichen, and Rosie visits a farm that is putting the value back into its Hebridean sheep fleeces.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Beechgrove Garden is looking ahead to summer days in the garden, with Lizzie Schofield back to work in the sitooterie area she started off last year as a place ideal for sitting out and enjoying the garden. This week, she prunes and plants in the same area to get it looking great for the good days ahead. Brian Cunningham is back to basics with a beginners' guide to starting off a productive veg plot with some spring sowing, and he also looks at what makes a great homemade compost. Lizzie takes on the tomato plants this year at Beechgrove, and she is looking at what varieties she has picked for 2026. She is also the first of the team to start work on her competition garden, which is based on planting that appeals to one of the senses. Brian is in the 6x8 greenhouse, and there is a trip to Dumfries for the latest from Colin Crosbie’s garden. This week's handy hints are all about what you should be doing with your dahlias right now.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Anna Jones brings a wealth of veggies to the table with her spicy take on a shepherd’s pie, adding the flavours of aloo gobi and saag aloo. Joe Wicks continues the pie theme with his delicious chicken, cauliflower and leek filo pastry pie with a side of purple sprouting broccoli. The formidable mother-daughter team Mumbai Mix present a taste of their delicious dosas, while Sabrina Ghayour celebrates Persian New Year with her crispy cod wraps and harissa mayo. Galton Blackiston uncovers the edible treasures of the Norfolk coast, from nettles to sea beets and scurvy grass to sea aster. Georgina Hayden combines two classic desserts for her delicious baklava cheesecake layered with filo pastry, walnuts and a feta filling. And Stacey Dooley tucks into either her food heaven, carpetbag steak, or her food hell, pigeon. Meanwhile, Rick Stein explores the English market in Cork, and Clive Myrie enjoys a tot of rum or two in Barbados.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three talented chefs from London and south east England are halfway through their heat, and the pressure is mounting. Once again, they each serve a unique take on the brief celebrating the British film industry and films with links to their region. The dishes are judged by veteran judge Aktar Islam. They include a 28-day dry-aged duck inspired by 28 Days Later, and a peanut and salted caramel parfait that serves as a tribute to the Baftas. But who will be leaving the competition?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from London and south east England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges: Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian and all-round food enthusiast Phil Wang. The guest judge is Simon Callow, the much-loved actor who has starred in such box office hits as Shakespeare in Love, A Room with a View and Four Weddings and a Funeral. How will he judge dishes celebrating the British film industry? Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent London and south east England at the national finals.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The British Motor Museum plays host to this programme, where experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley help the teams find bargains to sell for profit at auction.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims are in the Scottish Borders on St Cuthbert’s Way in the Cheviots. It takes them on a strenuous route up Wideopen Hill to reach some spectacular views. Later, the pilgrims head to Cuthbert’s Cave in the Kelso Hills, where legend says the monks rested with Cuthbert’s body when they fled Lindisfarne in the late 9th century in the wake of Viking raids. And on the last day of their pilgrimage, they follow the 5km walk across the tidal sands of Pilgrims' Way, where saints and pilgrims have trekked for over a thousand years. Finally, they arrive at Holy Island, where they reflect on a journey that has brought some surprising emotional revelations.

programme.title: Rear Window
programme.desc: Voyeuristic suspense thriller about a recuperating photojournalist who spends his time watching the neighbours from the confines of his own apartment and begins to become obsessed with the belief that he has witnessed a murder.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the lives of kingfishers, otters and other animals whose home is a river garden. Life here seems idyllic, but all are at the mercy of seasonal floods.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: A new generation of big cats is coming of age. First-time lion mum Serami’s tiny cubs surprise the crew and could mark a split in the Xudum pride. It’s time for Mathata and the oldest cubs in the pride to pull their weight. Pobe the cheetah makes a welcome return with her surviving cub Bo, who finally shows the team what he’s made of. Leopard mum Lediba’s female cub is becoming increasingly independent. But her new-found confidence panics her mum and the crew. And a new character bursts onto the scene, bringing even more tension to the delta.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Adam and Lucy have a bold idea to save on feed costs, but it means persuading their dairy cows to adopt beef calves and let them suckle. Emma and Rob are taking a holiday before the busy season kicks in, but first they need to get Emma's parents up to speed on running the farm. Ally helps a fellow crofter on an island gather, but low lamb numbers give him cause for concern about when to release his own crop of lambs back out to the hills.

programme.title: Olivier Awards 2026
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Nick Mohammed hosts the biggest night in theatre with a star-studded line-up at the Royal Albert Hall for the Olivier Awards' 50th anniversary. Highlights include performances from nominated musicals Paddington: The Musical, Evita, Into the Woods, The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry, Shucked and The Producers. Plus, spectacular celebrations for the 40th anniversary of The Phantom of the Opera and the 20th anniversary of Wicked. Dame Elaine Paige is honoured with this year’s Special Award, and contenders for other big awards include Cate Blanchett, Tom Hiddleston, Marianne Jean-Baptiste, Bryan Cranston, Giles Terera and Rachel Zegler. Paddington: The Musical and Into the Woods lead the way with 11 nominations each, but who will walk away with the night’s famous statuettes?

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: West Midlands Police are called after staff working in a tip yard make a grisly discovery: a decomposing body in a freezer which has been brought in for disposal following a flat clearance. A forensics team attends the scene, and the body and freezer are both removed for forensic tests. The team must first identify the body, a process that eventually leads the police to a surprising discovery: one of the deceased's friends has known about his death for quite some time.

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: Two unlikely partners in a bounty-hunting scam join forces in a new venture as they try to outdo a sadistic criminal looking for the $20,000 that a soldier has buried somewhere in the desert.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours of Saturday morning, a woman calls 999 – she’s been attacked and raped by a stranger in a public phone box in Birmingham. Police and forensic teams race to identify the attacker before he can strike again. While the victim is taken to hospital, a detailed examination of the phone box for evidence proves challenging, as it's contaminated with the fingerprints of so many members of the public. While detectives explore CCTV footage and a cluster of fingerprints, it's DNA evidence that may prove crucial.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Flashbacks show Stephen and Lisa’s first meeting, while Amanda’s pregnancy and Dave’s ambitions create uncertainty for all.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: At a friend’s wedding, Lisa struggles in the face of Amanda’s pregnancy, and Stephen tries to find the courage to confront Dave.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is in the driving seat in this special episode all about motoring memorabilia. In Oswestry, one team comes to an abrupt halt when they spot a vintage stop-go road sign. And in Exeter, another duo drives down the price of a luxury car mascot – with a magic trick! But which will cruise to the biggest profit at auction? Meanwhile, Eric journeys along the Time Road exhibition at the British Motor Museum to witness milestones in British motoring history. In the Cotswolds, Christina Trevanion goes for a spin at one of Britain’s most historic racing circuits. And in Solihull, Danny Sebastian is in his element as he sits astride some of the oldest, rarest and most valuable motorcycles in the world.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the lives of kingfishers, otters and other animals whose home is a river garden. Life here seems idyllic, but all are at the mercy of seasonal floods.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights from Sunday’s four Premier League games, including title contenders Manchester City against Chelsea. Elsewhere, Roberto De Zerbi takes charge of relegation-threatened Spurs for the first time against Sunderland, Nottingham Forest face Aston Villa, and Newcastle travel to Crystal Palace.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Clive Myrie’s Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Jamaica’s capital city, Clive learns he has hidden talents when it comes to trying out some dancehall moves. He explores a 19th-century mansion which tells the story of Jamaica’s first black millionaire and heads to the school that has produced most of the country’s Olympic sprinting talent. Clive also makes time to head for the hills to sample a special Jamaican-Irish stout punch as he learns of the long connection between these two islands.

programme.title: Nature's Epic Journeys
programme.desc: A hundred thousand caribou face starving bears and wolves, deadly frozen rivers and rugged mountains as they undertake the world's longest land migration - 5,000 kilometres through the frozen Arctic wilderness of Canada and Alaska. Liz Bonnin leads a team of scientists and film-makers using cutting-edge technology to follow the herd, providing insights into the individual lives of the animals. The caribou must reach their calving grounds before they give birth - failure would spell disaster.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A Steiff teddy bear bought 'for the dog to play with', a huge Chinese paper passport which saved a family's life in 1926, and a drug jar which Oliver Cromwell might have seen are among the items brought to the experts in Hartlepool. Plus, Michael Aspel is shown a piece of shrapnel which killed the owner's grandfather in the bombardment of Hartlepool in 1914.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog it! comes from Chiddingstone Castle in Kent. Antiques experts Thomas Plant and Adam Partridge find some fascinating antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Paul Martin visits Chartwell House, home of Sir Winston Churchill, to uncover the real man behind the politician.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins his journey in Japan’s dynamic second city, Osaka, famous for its food culture, its nightlife and its friendly people. Using the city’s metro system, he heads for the bustling Shinsekai neighbourhood to taste the city’s signature dish - fried octopus balls. Michael explores the artistic and cultural phenomenon of manga, the Japanese comics that have taken the world by storm, pays homage to the godfather of manga, Osamu Tezuka, and becomes the subject of his own railway manga. Michael’s next journey is on one of Japan’s world-famous bullet trains, the Shinkansen. He meets an engineer, ex-Shinkansen driver and now Japan Rail executive to find out about the bullet train’s amazing history and is blown away by the extraordinary next generation of trains, the maglev. Finally, Michael arrives in Nagoya, Japan’s manufacturing hub, and outside the city is a factory that makes one of the country’s most talked about exports - the smart loo.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, Farhat Javed looks at how Pakistan has positioned itself as a peace broker in the conflict between the US and Iran and BBC Eye investigates how routine visits for medical treatment in rural Pakistan have led to hundreds of children becoming infected with HIV, uncovering evidence of medical negligence and a failing public health system.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: Bill Bailey matches wildlife sanctuary owner Lindsay with celebrity artist and animal lover Ricky Wilson. But Ricky faces an artistic dilemma.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the last semi-final of the classic quiz where contenders return to the black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock to win the remaining place in the grand final. The specialist subjects are Amelia Earhart, Sir Laurence Olivier, the red wines of Burgundy and the songs of Sir Noël Coward.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second set of semi-finalists face off for a place in the final. Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes Made Me Me
programme.desc: Director Simon Draper sets out to make a podcast with actress Miriam Margolyes, but the project soon becomes sidetracked as Simon is drawn ever deeper into her orbit. The podcast becomes a chaotic, joyful road trip, revealing Miriam’s true legacy isn’t just her work but the lives she’s quietly transformed, beginning with Simon.

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: A single mum from Scotland's east coast embarks on a whirlwind romance with a Ghanaian preacher. They quickly marry, and she takes his side as the first lady of his church. But lives are torn apart when tragedy strikes. Reporter Myles Bonnar meets friends and family to try to find out what happened to Charmain.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: With unique access to friends and family, including Jackson’s sister La Toya, this episode charts Michael Jackson’s journey from child performer to a deeply troubled musical icon. Behind The Jackson 5, the family act that launched Michael to fame, was a household described by insiders as driven by fear as much as by talent. An offer of a film role helps Michael gain independence from his controlling father, causing tensions and resentments within the family. This leads to Michael's search for authentic connections elsewhere, including among the Jehovah's Witnesses. In 1982, Michael releases his masterpiece Thriller, catapulting him to megastardom and turning him into a global business empire. But the pressures of fame begin to weigh heavily, and colleagues also describe a pattern in Michael’s behaviour: a preference for the company of children.

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan explores Uganda, its people and culture, its past and its modern complexity, to find out whether Brits should add this this visually stunning, landlocked country the size of the UK to their holiday bucket list. With his guide, Bugandan princess Alex, Romesh learns about Uganda’s history before and after Idi Amin and how Uganda got its name, before sampling traditional rural village life by getting blind drunk on banana gin (of unknown alcohol strength). Rom is awed by Uganda’s natural beauty but is unable to ignore an ugly truth. Will the treatment of the LGBTQ+ community in Uganda in the wake of the widely supported and recently passed anti-gay laws force him to come to an uncomfortable conclusion about his time in the country?

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.desc: When a meteor shower is forecast to pass over Mossy Bottom, Bitzer really wants to see the shooting stars. However, he keeps getting distracted by the flock’s curiosity and the Farmer’s late-night picnic.

The girls' side becomes one big party with no rules, but on the boys' side Dog makes many rules! Boy and Mouse hate it, but they don't want to hurt Dog's feelings. Girl tries to lure them over to the girls' side with all the things they love to play with. Eventually Boy and Mouse sneak over to the girls' side to join in the fun - until Dog confronts them.

Callum may be Little Bro, but he’s in control, so Adam and Joe need to impress him! Adam and Joe wake up to discover Callum has made their arms grow extra long overnight. How will they walk the dog, make brekky and – most importantly – style their trademark locks with really long limbs? Once Callum decides who’s taking the win, it’s onto the Biggest Splash Challenge. Reluctantly trained to hunt the dragons that plague the village, Hiccup forms an unlikely friendship with Toothless, an injured dragon. Featuring the voices of Jay Baruchel (Hiccup Horrendous Haddock III), Gerard Butler (Stoick the Vast), Craig Ferguson (Gobber the Belch), America Ferrera (Astrid Hofferson), Jonah Hill (Snotlout), Christopher Mintz-Plasse (Fishlegs), T J Miller (Tuffnut), Kristen Wiig (Ruffnut), David Tennant (Spitelout), Ashley Jensen (Phlegma the Fierce), Robin Atkin Downes (Ack) and Philip McGrade (Stackard).

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Manic Pixie C*** Show: Animated comedy. Millie blackmails Louise into getting a bunch of collectible fairy wands from the annual Pixie Princess Promenade when she is banned from it. [S,AD] S12 Ep1

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Animated comedy about Bob Belcher, who runs Bob's Burgers restaurant, and his family. When Rudy's father Sylvester invites the Belchers to a bowling birthday party, Rudy is anxious that his mother's charismatic boyfriend Paul will upstage his father and harm his self-esteem and enlists the Belcher kids to make Sylvester look cool - elsewhere, Linda tries on some attractive bowling shoes while Bob tries to remember the details of the first time that she said 'I love you' to him.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Project Bakeover
programme.desc: Canadian reality series following renowned pastry chef, master chocolatier and entrepreneur Steve Hodge as he works with beloved design guru Tiffany Pratt to reinvigorate North American bakeries on the brink of losing it all. In the very first episode of the show, Steve and Tiffany visit Mrs Joy's Fabulous Treats in Lynchburg, Virginia, where bakery owner Tarsha is thinking about calling it quits.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Home improvement programme where contractor Brian McCourt and designer Sarah Keenleyside help to expand living spaces by tapping into people's backyards. Tina is a professional chef and amateur cake decorator - she and her husband Patrick have two young boys and a home that is overrun with both baking supplies and football equipment. To extend this family's living space, Brian and Sarah turn their backyard into something that is part-cake decorating studio and part- football field.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Home improvement programme where contractor Brian McCourt and designer Sarah Keenleyside help to expand living spaces by tapping into people's backyards. Kirsten and Joel have short commutes - Kirsten is a real estate agent who works from a home office while Joel is a sales representative with a makeshift workspace in their master bedroom. To help this couple reclaim their bedroom, Brian and Sarah look to the backyard for space to retrofit an old shipping container into an office.

programme.title: Buying the View
programme.desc: House-hunting show that explores luxury listings in majestic mountain ranges, sprawling beaches, and glittering urban skylines. When a father and daughter armed with a ten-million-dollar budget team up to buy a property that they can share in downtown Toronto, estate agent Jay Egan is confident that he can find them something that ticks off all the items on their wish list.

programme.title: The Only Way is Essex
programme.desc: The whole group attend a charity event to raise money for Young Epilepsy, a charity close to Becks' heart after being diagnosed with epilepsy following the birth of her first child - Diags hosts a quiz while Jordan hosts an auction where Wills is one of the lots up for grabs. Elsewhere, Harry and Junaid come face-to-face for the first time since Harry sent Joe and Junaid his make-up product, and Dan and Ella have a heart-to-heart and learn that things might not be quite as over as they thought.

programme.title: The Only Way is Essex
programme.desc: Essex's finest head to Barcelona for sun, fun and plenty of drama. Power couple Lockie and Yaz spend some quality time with their friends in the sun-soaked city and each admit that all is not well in their relationship. Loveable new boys Dean and Jordan have previous with most of Essex, but whose feathers will they ruffle most in Barcelona? Back in Essex, Gemma and Arg are taking bold strides in their relationship, and Pete bashfully admits to Chloe that he's going on a date.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: The iconic TV series is back, but in a brand-new setting with brand-new prize boxes and a new batch of contestants hoping to win a whopping cash prize - who will have what it takes to beat our brand-new Banker? Now hosted by Stephen Mulhern, the series will see contestants battle it out against the notorious Banker to be in with a chance of winning a life-changing cash prize in a nail-biting game of nerves and intuition. Contestants are confronted with 22 sealed boxes with each one hiding a different amount of money - without knowing what each one contains, the contestant must eliminate the boxes, which are opened one at a time, and the amount of money inside is revealed. Pressure mounts as the player is tempted by the notorious Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for what might be inside their box, and as long as the larger cash prizes remain unchosen, the Banker's offers will get higher... but that can all change in an instant if a big cash prize is unveiled.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal

programme.title: Sing
programme.desc: Animated comedy about the story of a koala called Buster Moon, a theatre impresario who has one chance to restore his theatre to its former glory - Buster launches a singing competition when his establishment starts struggling and is on the verge of being lost forever, but the contest soon becomes grander than he could have ever imagined and changes the lives of everyone involved...

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The auditions continue as further acts take to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals? They are all hoping to secure a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the world-famous Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: Peter Pan
programme.desc: Fantasy adventure. Wendy Darling mesmerises her brothers in stifling Edwardian London every night with bedtime tales of swordplay, swashbuckling and the fearsome Captain Hook, but the children become the heroes of an even greater story when Peter Pan flies into their nursery one night and leads them over moonlit rooftops through a galaxy of stars and to the lush jungles of Neverland. Here the children join Peter and the Lost Boys in an exhilarating life... and an inevitable showdown with Hook.

programme.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
programme.desc: Heart-warming sci-fi adventure. After a gentle alien becomes stranded on Earth, the being is discovered and befriended by a young boy called Elliott. Bringing the extra-terrestrial into his suburban California house, Elliott calls the alien E.T. and introduces it to his brother Michael and his little sister Gertie - the children decide to keep its existence a secret. When E.T. falls ill, however, the US government intervenes - and a dire situation follows for both Elliott and the alien.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. When a drunk Lois nags Peter into winning her a carnival prize at Oktoberfest by knocking down milk bottles with a ball, Peter is unable to throw the ball properly and is mocked by his friends and family who insult his masculinity - meanwhile, Brian gets a chiropractic adjustment at the event and decides to go into the business with Stewie after feeling ripped off by the expensive bill he gets for it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. When a sex-related injury leaves Jerome confined to a wheelchair, Peter and the guys decide to raise money for his restorative surgery by shooting a sexy men-in-uniform calendar - the plan is a success, but when it's discovered that Cleveland accidentally exposed himself while posing, he is fired from his job. Peter then takes Cleveland to his job, only to end up being demoted when Cleveland is taken on there.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The men go to a ranch in order to regain their sense of masculinity. Elsewhere, Stewie has an accident on a slide that leads him to think he's started having periods.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: US animated comedy series. Roger turns the Smiths' house into a restaurant that serves jambalaya against Francine's wishes, Stan and Hayley go on a road trip to stalk a woman on Facebook, and Steve and Klaus write a film script starring John Cena.

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Sitcom about three desperate young men who reside in ancient Rome. When the boys get a dose of culture after Grumio is street-cast to play the part of Cupid in an upcoming play, Marcus becomes his agent and uses his new connections to impress Delphine - meanwhile, Stylax understudies the lead actor so he can meet some theatre groupies.

As one relationship teeters in the balance, another one blossoms as perpetual bachelor Dan wants to give things a go with new hottie, Clelia.

programme.title: The Only Way is Essex
programme.desc: Reality series. As Pete's new relationship with Shelby goes from strength to strength, his friends are concerned that he's isolating himself from the rest of the group. There'll be just one question to answer...

programme.title: Deal or No Deal Celebrity Special

programme.title: Celebrity Sabotage
programme.desc: Get ready for a brand-new and hilarious reality game show that's jam-packed with all your favourite celebrities! Each week Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry are set secret missions to sabotage the unsuspecting contestants of totally fake TV shows, all to build up a secret prize pot! Along the way they'll be joined by special celebrity guests from Clare Balding to Rylan Clark and Harry Redknapp. This episode sees the celebs take on 'SOS: Survive or Surrender', a fake military-themed show hosted by Former Special Forces Commando Jason Fox, who is totally in on the whole thing. But that's not all as actor and comedian Guz Khan joins Mission HQ to get involved with all the mayhem as this week's rebellious Guest Saboteur. Expect chaos, carnage and calamity!

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The auditions continue as further acts take to the stage with performances to impress the crowd and judges. Which acts will win themselves a coveted space in the semi-finals? They are all hoping to secure a prize of £250,000 and a sought-after slot at the world-famous Royal Variety Performance.

programme.title: New: Britain's Got Talent: Unseen
programme.desc: KSI takes part in Tu Strange's wonderfully wacky audition. Plus, schoolteacher Ben and his remarkable students inspire the audience with their uplifting original song. [S] S3 Ep7

programme.title: Liar Liar
programme.desc: Broad slapstick comedy. Fletcher Reade is the fastest, loudest, and brashest lawyer in town - he has a habit of lying to ensure his success and that includes lying to his divorced wife Audrey and even to his son Max. When Fletcher misses his son's birthday party after yet another lie, Max wishes that his dad could tell the truth for 24 hours - a magical force then makes the wish come true and Fletcher cannot stand the results. To make matters worse for him, Fletcher is working on a case that could send him to the big leagues of law - naturally, lying is the only way that he can make it a successful one. Unable to force the lies out of his mouth, Fletcher must come to terms with what Max has done and understand why he did so... and he must do so sooner rather than later as Audrey is planning to move away from Fletcher with Max and start a new life with her new love interest Jerry.

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered how clever you are? This is your chance to find out as comedy legend Lee Mack presents the ultimate leveller of a quiz show - it doesn't matter if you're not usually good at quizzes because the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts and are all about logic and common sense. This is the quiz that the whole family can play together - 100 contestants begin every show and try to make it to the end to answer a question that only one per cent of the country can get right and win up to 100,000 pounds.

programme.title: TikTok: Murder Gone Viral
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring extraordinary true crime cases linked to the world's fastest-growing social media platform. In a case that has drawn millions of views on TikTok, 17-year-old Charlie Cosser was fatally stabbed by 16-year-old Yura Varybrus at an end-of-term summer party for teenagers - Charlie didn't know Yura but had asked him to leave after a girl said that he had been bothering her. As Charlie lay dying, his dad Martin made him a promise that he would campaign for justice and raise awareness of knife crime - Martin's agonised, angry and tender videos tell the story of a family struggling to come to terms with an act of senseless violence that tore their lives apart.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. While at the Halloween Carnival with his family and friends, Peter stumbles across the owner of a hologram-themed attraction who shows off a device that can create interactive holograms - after bribing the inventor, Peter decides to make a hologram based on the best version of himself to get out of menial tasks. While Peter enjoys its usefulness at first, he grows jealous after seeing his hologram kiss Lois...

programme.title: Family Guy

Meanwhile, Peter and Lois successfully bid on tickets to Vermont at the fundraiser and set out to break Bonnie's Instagram like record by taking a scenic photo.

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Detective drama series. Pursued by the press, under the spotlight of an internal investigation, and lacking support from his superiors, Wycliffe's problems multiply when an old case resurfaces and DCC Stevens seems out to prove that he is no longer up to the job.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length drama following on from the Inspector Morse series. When north Oxford housewife Rachel Mallory is found hanged in her home leaving two children and a devastated husband behind, the initial investigation suggests suicide but Lewis's instincts tell him that something is wrong with the verdict - when he strikes up a friendship with the Mallorys' neighbour Stephanie Fielding, she points him in the direction of a key piece of evidence.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway are sent upon a seemingly impossible quest when botanist Liv Nash accidentally digs up the body of the recently-buried professor Murray Hawes - Murray was a man possessed and fixated upon solving an impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll, but could his obsession have been dangerous enough to get him killed?

programme.title: Goodnight Mister Tom
programme.desc: World War II drama in which a curmudgeonly widower living in a small village takes in a painfully shy evacuee from London. The widower soon realises that his young charge has been damaged both mentally and physically at the hands of his strict mother. He sets about undoing the damage, teaching the youngster to read, write and draw. But then the child is summoned back to the city.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Alan King, co-owner of King’s Crystal with his brother Charles, is killed in a car accident in Shanghai during a business trip with his brother Charles and accountant Peter Baxter - the company faces bankruptcy and the workforce, which is led by volatile glassblower Jack Tewson, is devastated. Tensions are brewing throughout Midsomer Magna - as Alan’s son Ian shuns his mother Hilary to grieve with his close friend David Monroe, Sophie Baxter, the daughter of Peter, is in love with Ian while undertaker James Mason is in love with Sophie. Meanwhile, Cully is rehearsing for a production of Hamlet at the Midsomer Open Air Theatre which has been refurbished thanks to Alan’s fundraising - as Barnaby watches Cully in the performance of Hamlet, he realises that the play contains a message sent by Ian King before he died. Can the detective pick up the trail of clues to lead him to the killer?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is impressed when blues guitarist Jack 'Axeman' McKinley rolls into Causton with his waiflike daughter Willow for a reunion of the band Hired Gun at the Midsomer Rocks Festival - drummer Nicky Harding, alcoholic diva Mimi Clifton and lead singer Gary Cooper join him, but Gary becomes paranoid after finding a dead sheep, a disembowelled pig, and a skinned rabbit. When Mimi drops dead, could a missing band member hold the key... or will Barnaby find that one of his heroes is a murderer?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: David Mostyn is shocked to find his mother Delyth in bed with Dr James Kirkwood, a senior partner in the Midsomer Market surgery, but the beautiful divorcee is delighted when James uses the occasion to propose to her - her daughter Megan, James' practice manager, is equally outraged and especially when Delyth tells her they are planning to sell up and travel the world. James also has the difficult task of telling Dorothy Hutton, his late wife's mother who is still grieving, and asks his surgery partner Alan Delaney to check on Dorothy while lending him his brand-new Range Rover for the journey - when Alan has to leave the car during a storm, a pick-up truck drives into him at speed and kills him. Barnaby and Jones dig into Alan's past and discover that James once accused him of stealing 30,000 pounds from the practice, but it was put down to clerical error and the two doctors made up - as Barnaby and Jones investigate, they find that they must travel to Snowdonia to unravel the truth.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Rivalry between traditional film photographers and digital camera fans in the village of Luxton Deeping reaches fever pitch at an annual photographic society exhibition when snapper Steve Bright threatens black and white film enthusiast Lionel Bell - Lionel is then photographing his favourite oak trees when he is strangled with the cord from his light meter. Lionel's diary shows that Barnaby visited him the night he died and a glass with his fingerprints is found... but is he being framed?

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The very first episode of the detective drama series following two detectives who seemingly have nothing in common but are thrown together and forge a rumbustious partnership in Bath. When a man is shot in the deserted mansion of one of Bath's most prominent industrialists - the inventor Maxton Crockett - newly-arrived DCI McDonald and DS Dodds unravel a tangled web of lies as they attempt to find the identity of the killer.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Police drama series. A 20-year-old missing persons case is reopened when the remains of a teenage girl are finally found - with the mother desperate to discover the truth about her daughter's disappearance, Frost attempts to get to the bottom of a case that has spanned two decades.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Classic sitcom about a bus company. Stan takes Olive to the divorce courts as Arthur has now been gone for six months.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jonny is shocked when Katie tells him of her plot to cut Gray out of the baby's life. Pollard has a visit from a health and safety officer, and Louise attempts to apologise to Viv.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Detective drama series. A prostitute sparks a murder hunt when she tells Kersey that one of her clients confessed to killing another prostitute, but how believable is she?

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: West Country detective drama series. Wycliffe is recovered and back at work when a local climber is killed in a fall from a cliff - an unreliable informant claims that he was pushed, but a re-enactment of the fatal climb only adds to suspicions.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the murder of a professor of German history who had a fascination with local underground boxing bouts - when they learn that one of his students was the latest winner of one fight, they suspect that the murder may have connections to the underworld.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A rape case appears to be solved when the chief suspect is murdered, but nothing is what it seems and Lewis and Hathaway soon find the upper echelons of Oxford society closing ranks - meanwhile, Lewis is reminded of his wife's tragic death.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series about a GP who relocates to the sleepy Cornish hamlet of Portwenn. Martin meets the village's new midwife Molly as she will be looking after Louisa, but Martin is dismissive of her profession and the two of them do not hit it off - meanwhile, Bert and Al have a new cook at their restaurant, called Marigold, who surprises them both one morning by bringing in some dead pheasants that she claims to have caught in a snare. When Marigold cuts her finger and Bert protectively rushes her to the surgery, Marigold tells Martin that her sister has glandular fever and Martin orders her to take extra hygiene precautions around the food she cooks - elsewhere, Martin visits Louisa's school to perform hearing assessments on the children when Louisa reveals that she is having 'toilet troubles'. Martin is quick to diagnose an urinary tract infection and writes a prescription for her, but Molly advocates a more natural holistic approach - whose treatment method will win the day?

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series about a GP who relocates to the sleepy Cornish hamlet of Portwenn. Joan starts a small bed and breakfast business to solve her farm's money problems - her first guest is local farmer Ted, who has also got into financial difficulties. They're a good match initially, but Joan soon finds that Ted is far from the perfect guest when he has a suspected mild heart attack at her farm and Martin finds a mysterious lump in his abdomen - elsewhere, Pauline confronts Martin about his contract offer for the London job. When Pauline ends up being ejected from the surgery while trying to demonstrate her worth, an impromptu afternoon by the seaside with Al goes wrong when she is stung by a jellyfish and must seek out Martin for help. Meanwhile, Louisa finds it hard to enjoy her baby shower because everyone wants to talk about Martin leaving Portwenn, and the Doc joins Edith at a hotel where she is giving the keynote speech to a medical conference about her research.

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Light-hearted drama series about a pair of gardening enthusiasts whose amateur sleuthing involves them in all kinds of mysteries. Horticultural lecturer Rosemary Boxer is brought to Winterbourne Manor, the beautiful home of wealthy but ill businessman Daniel Kellaway, to help investigate a disease that is causing trees to lose their bark - meanwhile, Laura Thyme has left her adulterous husband Nick and has ended up at the house of Sam Trent, Nick's best friend and Daniel's business partner. When Sam suffers a suspicious fatal car accident and Rosemary is dismissed from her college, Laura and Rosemary turn to each other for mutual support - one death is almost followed by another when the girls return to Winterbourne to find Daniel lying unconscious beneath his bedroom window. Did his illness drive him to attempt to take his own life, or was he pushed? As Rosemary and Laura solve the case and save the trees, they discover a poisonous weed and a housekeeper with a murky past...

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Light-hearted drama series about a pair of gardening enthusiasts-turned-amateur sleuths. Rosemary and Laura are hired to create a wild garden at pop star Nev Connolly's grandiose Gothic pile where a set of horse bones is discovered - while writing music late at night, Nev hears noises and sets out to investigate. After Rosemary is woken by gunshots and the sound of a car screeching away, she finds Nev shot dead on the grass - digging the next day, Rosemary and Laura discover another skeleton that is human. Rosemary calls the police, but not before pocketing a piece of evidence - a coat toggle which is lying next to the hand - as the friends unearth a complex mystery from years ago... and come under fire themselves.

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Detective drama series following two detectives who seemingly have nothing in common but are thrown together and forge a rumbustious partnership in Bath. When a young woman dies in a busy park in broad daylight, the uncovering of her identity leads DS Dodds to confront secrets from his own past.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a murder is announced in the personal columns of the Chipping Cleghorn Gazette, the locals wonder if it is just a game. The curious villagers then assemble at Lettie Blacklock's house to find that their host did not place the advert; the room goes dark and three gun shots ring out. Lettie is injured in the process, but the man who fired the gun ends up dead. Miss Marple pays a visit to her friend Amy Murgatroyd and sets about uncovering the dark secrets of Chipping Cleghorn.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Nick Cheyney - the director of a new film version of The Scarlet Pimpernel - is beheaded by a guillotine on set, there is no shortage of suspects, They include an actor whose career was ruined by Cheyney, a woman who bore his child after a drunken one-night stand, and an embittered extra - when medical evidence shows that Cheyney was drugged with morphine before he died, can Barnaby and Jones find the killer before the case becomes as bloody as the French Revolution?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a local ne'er-do-well arouses suspicions due to the complexity of his death.

programme.title: Code of Silence (Signed Version)
programme.desc: Suspenseful crime drama. Alison's informant work grows riskier as she secretly follows Braden and Liam to a building site - using a speech-to-text app, she eavesdrops on Helen and the gang planning the heist and learns that they need an insider to access the vault. Alison shares the intelligence with the police and eventually identifies the gang's target as billionaire Amir Joshi, who intends to auction a priceless necklace in London - she soon discovers that her and Julie's looming eviction is tied to gang member Joseph Holhurst, pushing her to become increasingly conflicted about her bond with Liam. As the police find evidence that Braden may be buying guns, Alison confronts Liam about being involved in illegal activities - Liam swears that he would never hurt anyone, but Liam realises that he can't keep that promise when Braden turns up with a gun in Liam's flat and threatens him.

programme.title: Code of Silence (Signed Version)
programme.desc: Suspenseful crime drama. Alison's life as a police informant becomes precarious when she is invited onto a lavish trip to a luxurious hotel with Liam - Liam insists on adopting false identities and Alison soon discovers that he is using her as cover to spy on Amir Joshi and his family. Alison is convinced that she can walk the fine line between her duty to the police and their undeniable connection - it's when Liam opens up about his troubled past as a foster child and the pressures driving his involvement in the robbery that Alison wavers. She's falling in love with him and can't resist it any longer when they get back to their room - when she gets back, Alison lies to the detectives about sleeping with Liam. When it is revealed that Liam is listening in having hacked Alison's hearing aids, Liam doesn't confront Alison about her betrayal - and instead shows her the necklace he plans to steal as he proposes that they flee with his ill-gotten wealth to build a future after the heist.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan is having difficulty supporting the whole family on his meagre earnings.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jonny is shocked when Katie tells him of her plot to cut Gray out of the baby's life. Pollard has a visit from a health and safety officer, and Louise attempts to apologise to Viv.

programme.title: Never the Twain
programme.desc: Classic British sitcom. When burglars go to work in the neighbourhood, this leads to an eventful night for Oliver, Simon, Mrs Sadler and Banks when they are held at gunpoint and some interesting characteristics come to light in the process...

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: There is a celebration at Luxton and District because it is 50 years since they got their first motor bus. Blakey organises an exhibition to re-create the "good old days" before the Unions got hold of the busmen and made his life impossible.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liam faces up to his night with Carla, while Maria tells him she wants to register the baby's death and Gail has a run-in with Tina's mum when she turns up at the Platts'.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rosie catches Liam sharing a kiss with Carla and films them on her mobile phone. Maria has a change of heart about her separation and Julie drives Fiz to breaking point.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Detective drama. When Lewis and Hathaway are called in to investigate the murder of a rising star on the Oxford theatrical circuit, they focus their suspicions on the cast of his current play - as Lewis discovers all the theatrical ambitions and jealousies and wonders if an actor would kill for a good part, Hathaway thinks he may have uncovered just who killed Lewis's wife in a hit and run accident years before.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Police drama series set in a 1960s Yorkshire village. An American motorcyclist called Charlie turns up in Aidensfield and causes quite a stir when Kate has to fix him up after an accident, Gina falls for him, and a local Korean War veteran decides to pick a fight with him! As things get slightly out of hand, Gina has to decide whether to accept Charlie's offer of a move to London or not - meanwhile, Greengrass acquires a couple of classic English motorbikes claiming that they are for a regimental museum... but in fact he plans to sell them on to Charlie!

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Drama series set in 1960s Yorkshire about a policeman. Inspector Crossley from Whitby arrives at the police house to see Nick but is angry to find the police house being used as a doctor's surgery by Kate - meanwhile, a new job offer could be a fresh start for Kate, but Nick is reluctant for her to accept. As a coach trip to Whitby organised by Gina becomes a welcome day out for the locals and Kate and Nick are dragged into a desperate situation when a little girl loses her father on the beach, Greengrass is in trouble again... and this time it's for years of tax evasion.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When council clerk Martin Barrett is shot dead, Barnaby and Jones discover that he was an expert blackmailer. They investigate which of his victims hated him enough to kill him, and the trail leads to Fennacombe Bay. Why are Richard and Maggie Florian spending so much time at sea - and could police committee chairman Sir John Waverley have a scandalous past? With the help of a former policeman turned fisherman, Barnaby takes to the waves to find out.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nostalgic police drama. Greengrass is ordered to have Alfred destroyed, but Nick suspects that another party may be to blame and has to prove his theory before Alfred pays for the crime.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Police drama series set in the 1960s. programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Napoleonic War-based action-adventure. June 1813 - the South Essex is seriously short of men, and when the Duke of Wellington is told that there are no recruits available, he sends Sharpe to England to investigate. When Sharpe and Harper arrive, they are informed that there are 700 men waiting for them in Chelmsford - however, Sharpe finds the barracks deserted with no sign of his missing regiment. He and Harper decide to assume other identities and enlist - recruited by Sergeant Havercamp, Sharpe and Harper arrive at a training camp near the estate of Sir Henry Simmerson where they are trained mercilessly. Sharpe discovers that the new recruits are being auctioned to other regiments and returns to the Horse Guards with this information to entrust it to an old friend called Lawford... but Lawford wants to avoid a scandal and promptly tells Lord Fenner, who tries to have Sharpe killed. With the help of Fenner's mistress Lady Anne, Sharpe must now find the proof to implicate Fenner.

programme.desc: The 13th entry in the James Bond film series. 007 is asked to investigate the murder of a fellow agent who was clutching a priceless Faberge egg at the time of his death - the trail leads to the mysterious Octopussy, whose travelling circus features a company of gorgeous and athletic women. Bond and Octopussy share a passionate attraction, but 007 soon discovers that the elegant exiled Afghan prince Kamal Khan is working with a mad Soviet officer to hurl mankind into World War III. As Bond tries to stop the nightmarish scheme, his exploits include a tense chase through the streets of India, a deadly brawl on top of a speeding train, and a treacherous mid-air knife fight on an airplane wing.

programme.title: Smurfs: The Lost Village
programme.desc: Smurfette and her friends go to the Forbidden Forest to find a lost village. When the evil wizard Gargamel gets to know of this, he devises a plan to stop the smurfs from discovering the town.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Across The Spider-Verse
programme.desc: Miles travels the multiverse with Gwen, teaming up with Spider-People to fight the villain Spot while facing multiverse dangers and complex rules in this epic adventure.

programme.title: The Mitchells Vs The Machines
programme.desc: The Mitchells, a quirky, dysfunctional family, must put aside their differences and fight a robot apocalypse during their long-awaited road trip, all while trying to save humanity.

programme.title: Cloudy With A Chance Of Meatballs
programme.desc: A scientist tries to solve world hunger only to see things go awry as food falls from the sky in abundance.

programme.title: The Adventures of Tintin

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: Transformania
programme.desc: A science experiment goes wrong: Johnny becomes a monster while others turn human. Now the group must find a way to reverse it before it's permanent.

programme.title: Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween
programme.desc: A spooky adventure where kids face mysterious creatures unleashed from a magical book, leading to thrilling encounters and a race to save their town.

programme.title: Jumanji: The Next Level
programme.desc: Adventurers return to the magical game world where friendship, courage, and teamwork lead to thrilling escapes.

programme.title: The Water Horse
programme.desc: During WWII, Angus finds a mysterious egg that hatches a water horse. He protects the growing creature from soldiers and danger, forming a deep bond in this magical adventure.

programme.title: Harold & The Purple Crayon
programme.desc: Harold, who can make drawings come alive in a book, becomes a real-world adult and learns he has to understand more about everyday real life.

programme.title: Salt
programme.desc: A CIA agent goes on the run after a defector accuses her of being a Russian spy.

programme.title: Premium Rush

programme.title: The Walk
programme.desc: With help from a specially recruited crew, high-wire artist Petit walks the void between the World Trade Center towers.

programme.title: The Karate Kid
programme.desc: A troubled teen learns discipline and courage through martial arts training, finding purpose and respect under an unconventional mentor.

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: Spider-Man seeks Doctor Strange's aid to erase his identity exposure, but the spell malfunctions, unleashing dangerous multiversal threats upon them.

programme.title: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions
programme.desc: Six people are captured in a perilous game of escape rooms. To survive, they must team up with two original survivors, but as the rounds go, they wonder if this is a game they've been through before.

programme.title: Blade Runner 2049
programme.desc: Officer K of the LAPD discovers a dangerous secret and embarks on a mission to find a missing former blade runner who vanished over thirty years ago, risking everything to uncover the truth.

programme.title: Sisu
programme.desc: When an ex-soldier who discovers gold in the Lapland wilderness tries to take the loot into the city, Nazi soldiers led by a brutal SS officer battle him.

programme.title: The Cable Guy
programme.desc: A lonely and disturbed cable TV installer aims to ruin the life of a customer who rejects his attempts at friendship.

programme.title: Monster Hunter
